item.title: Superyacht linked to Putin's friend slips through Strait of Hormuz
item.description: It's the most talked-about and dangerous stretch of ocean in the world but, at the weekend, a superyacht linked to one of Russia's richest men made it through the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: First Nations film Wolfram writes hope into heavy history
item.description: Convinced to return to the story of the 2018 outback drama Sweet Country, Kaytetye filmmaker Warwick Thornton fell in love with the possibilities.

item.title: Travel quiz, April 29: Grab your ticket and a map, we're off on a quiz adventure
item.description: The Galápagos Islands are part of which South American country? The Elgin Marbles were taken from which ancient site?

item.title: Worried about a neighbour's dog? Here's what you can do
item.description: An unhappy dog doesn't necessarily mean criminal neglect, even if the level of care is below what you would give your own pet.

item.title: Amount of antibiotic florfenicol used at Tasmanian salmon farm lease concerning, say Greens, expert
item.description: Almost 3 tonnes of the antibiotic florfenicol were used at one Tasmanian salmon farm lease in just two months, with information on its use across eight other sites yet to be released to the public by authorities.

item.title: Fuel security a 'tricky balancing act', experts say as budget nears
item.description: As the international fuel crisis continues to cause concern, the federal government is under pressure to provide a fix during next month's budget.

item.title: Former ASIO chief questioned scrutiny of Bondi gunmen before quitting royal commission
item.description: While advising the royal commission examining the Bondi Beach terrorist attack, the former head of Australia's domestic intelligence agency questioned whether early assessments of gunman Naveed Akram were sufficiently rigorous.

item.title: UAE exit from OPEC another disruption to the fragile oil market
item.description: The United Arab Emirates is set to leave the Organization of the Petroleum Exporting Countries, creating yet another disruption to the global oil market. At home, Treasurer Jim Chalmers says the Middle East war is to blame for a rise in inflation.

item.title: King Charles addresses joint sitting of US Congress
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Early Farrer voters make choices known as pre-polling gets underway in blockbuster by-election
item.description: Pre-polling commences in the seat of Farrer for the hotly contested by-election triggered by the resignation of former opposition leader Sussan Ley.

item.title: Marcus Stoinis hits half-century in Punjab Kings' six-wicket IPL loss to Rajasthan Royals
item.description: Ricky Ponting's Punjab Kings record a six-wicket loss against Rajasthan Royals in the IPL, despite Marcus Stoinis's breathtaking innings.

item.title: Hundreds of jobs to go at Home Affairs department in voluntary redundancy drive
item.description: The secretary of the second-largest department says she has been trying to reduce headcount by natural attrition, but that further job cuts are needed by the end of the financial year to fit within the department's budget.

item.title: Lions star Oscar Allen sidelined for 12-14 weeks with foot injury
item.description: The Lions' win over the Crows on Sunday has come at a price, with one of their major off-season signings rule out for much of the season.

item.title: Family of Queensland grandmother who died after weight-loss surgery says her death was preventable
item.description: Joanne Downie underwent an elective gastric bypass surgery so she could live a healthier life for her grandchildren, but she died six days later in a regional Queensland hospital.

item.title: VicGrid surveyors cancel access visit after farmgate protest makes them feel unsafe
item.description: Renewable planning body VicGrid has threatened a high-profile anti-transmission-line campaigner with enforcement action after government surveyors felt "hindered" from accessing his farm.

item.title: US regulator orders review of ABC licence after Donald Trump criticises Jimmy Kimmel
item.description: It comes after both Donald and Melania Trump demanded the network cancel Kimmel's late-night comedy show over a joke they described as a call to violence, days before an alleged attempt to assassinate the US president.

item.title: Jarome Luai confirms Wests Tigers exit to join PNG Chiefs for 2028 NRL season
item.description: Jarome Luai will leave Wests Tigers at the end of the 2027 season, announcing he will join the PNG Chiefs for their entry to the NRL.

item.title: New theatre hopes to attract audiences as remote Queensland town grapples with tourism crisis
item.description: A potentially 'catastrophic' drop in traffic has outback residents concerned for the rest of the tourist season. Community members in remote Queensland have come together for the grand opening of a new outdoor theatre and a ribbon-cutting ceremony, declaring their town open for business.

item.title: Rebel Wilson accused of dumping her phone to avoid handing over key texts as part of defamation trial
item.description: Ms Wilson's lawyers have previously told Federal Court defamation hearing that her phone was stolen and she was unable to produce all of her WhatsApp message history.

item.title: Headline inflation surges to 4.6 per cent in March as higher fuel prices bite
item.description: Headline inflation surges to 4.6 per cent in March, but underlying inflation remains at 3.3 per cent.

item.title: Twenty-five-million-year-old platypus fossils with teeth found in outback SA
item.description: Researchers have discovered ancient platypus fossils in outback South Australia, where they are believed to have lived with lungfish, flamingos and freshwater dolphins. They also had major a difference from the modern species: well-formed teeth.

item.title: Keith Lees faces court over alleged murder of Meaghan Louise Rose on Sunshine Coast in 1997
item.description: Police allege Keith Lees killed Meaghan Louise Rose, whose body was found at the base of cliffs on the Sunshine Coast in July 1997.

item.title: Australia's richest person donates 'sexy' $1 million plane to Pauline Hanson's One Nation
item.description: One Nation leader Pauline Hanson banks more than $3 million worth of donations, including a brand-new plane gifted by Australia's richest person, Gina Rinehart.

item.title: WA farmers seeding their 'riskiest crop in years' welcome early rain
item.description: Widespread rain delivers timely relief to grain growers across the southern Wheatbelt, as they balance seeding decisions against soaring diesel and fertiliser costs.

item.title: AI deepfakes are circulating in our schools. What's the right way to handle them?
item.description: Deepfake content, made from artificial intelligence, is increasingly circulating in schools. But what can parents do if their children are the target?

item.title: Inflation details may give RBA pause for thought on interest rates
item.description: While inflation remains well above the Reserve Bank's target, there are details in the numbers that may give it reasons to hold off another rate hike in May.

item.title: The six weeks from hell that whipped New South Wales into State of Origin shape
item.description: For the second year in a row both New South Wales and Queensland enter the women's State of Origin match with barely a game between them this year, but a six-week training block that one player called "one of the hardest things I've ever done" has them primed for the series opener.

item.title: AFL confirms draft changes despite 'disingenuous' opposition from clubs
item.description: The AFL is pushing ahead with draft and player-movement changes for this off-season, with Carlton president Rob Priestly and Western Bulldogs coach Luke Beveridge criticising the move.

item.title: FIFA allows Afghan women's football team to return to international competition
item.description: Afghan women footballers will be able to showcase their skills to the world for the first time in five years after FIFA cleared the way for their return to international competition.

item.title: Threat to homes eases as fire near Uralla in NSW downgraded
item.description: The threat posed to homes by a grass fire burning on the state's Northern Tablelands eases after earlier prompting an Emergency alert.

item.title: High fuel prices hurting regional families taking kids to sporting fixtures
item.description: Country sporting clubs are rallying to help families attend fixtures as fuel prices double, with some travelling up to 640 kilometres. But it is an extra cost many will take on to maintain the sports are so integral to their communities.

item.title: Inflation surges to highest level since 2023
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: UK police declare terrorist incident after two Jewish men stabbed in London
item.description: A man has been arrested after two men were stabbed in London in an area with a large Jewish population.

item.title: APRA music awards 2026 winners announced, with Amyl and the Sniffers taking top gong
item.description: Punk rockers Amyl and The Sniffers have bagged three top gongs at the Australian music industry's APRA awards.

item.title: China inching towards resuming fuel exports as Penny Wong presses Australia's case
item.description: Chinese state-owned oil companies have agreed to negotiate directly with Australian companies on jet fuel after the foreign minister's visit to China.

item.title: Pauline Hanson billed taxpayers for flights to fundraisers and travel to catch Rinehart jet to Florida
item.description: One Nation leader Pauline Hanson billed taxpayers for two trips that included party fundraisers, discussions with disaffected National Barnaby Joyce, a Gina Rinehart event and return flights to connect the senator to her private jet to Florida, sponsored by her major donor, Mrs Rinehart.

item.title: US Supreme Court curbs race-based voting maps in landmark ruling
item.description: The ruling will be seen by civil rights activists as a blow and by the Republican party as a boost ahead of key midterm elections in November.

item.title: See how the price of everything has changed with inflation
item.description: If it feels like everything is getting more expensive, that's because it is. With the rate of inflation rising this week, we've tracked how the price of everyday items has increased.

item.title: Coalition's risky Farrer strategy could clear path for One Nation
item.description: Pauline Hanson is now set to tour more regional Coalition-held seats in her "sexy" new private plane, and the Liberal and National parties are helping clear the runway.

item.title: Flag quiz, part 1: Are you a vexillologist in the making?
item.description: Do you know the Union Jack from the Stars and Stripes? And which flags aren't rectangular?

item.title: Matthew Pavlich and Steven Baker reflect on the infamous 'Sirengate' match in Launceston 20 years on
item.description: Two decades ago, two teams met at York Park in Launceston. The siren sounded but the match continued and the dreaded word "gate" was added to another footy scandal.

item.title: The vine with a murky past that is strangling Vanuatu's forests
item.description: A habitat-destroying invasive species is taking over Vanuatu's forests, but one village is fighting back and hoping to earn an income for its people at the same time.

item.title: Will the OPEC oil cartel survive without the UAE?
item.description: The United Arab Emirates decision to pull the plug on its OPEC membership could be the nail in the coffin for an organisation that has struggled to keep its members in line for the past 66 years.

item.title: Technology should make life easier but it doesn't always for people with disability
item.description: While the newest home technology aims to make life easier, when it prioritises aesthetics over usability, some lives are actually made much harder, writes Nas Campanella.

item.title: New study suggests majority of regional Australians back renewable energy transition
item.description: A new research study indicates a majority of regional Australians support the renewable transition despite some protests over planned infrastructure.

item.title: War deepens oil crisis and pushes inflation higher — as it happened
item.description: The price of Brent crude has surged to over $US120 per barrel because US President Donald Trump has announced he will extend the naval blockade of the strait.

item.title: Six WA Police officers sanctioned over treatment of Aboriginal man who died in Kalgoorlie prison
item.description: Six WA Police officers have been sanctioned over their treatment of a man in their custody who later died in prison, after the ABC revealed a constable dismissed his threat of suicide, and laughed at news of his death.

item.title: ASX falls as oil price hits four-year high of $US126 — as it happened
item.description: The Australian share market traded lower as the oil price surged above $US125 in afternoon trading. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

item.title: Australians urged to stand up for Murray River as 'transformational moment' looms
item.description: Julie Jones has lived by the River Murray all her life and wants all South Australians to participate in the first comprehensive review of the Murray-Darling Basin Plan.

item.title: Travis Head helps Sunrisers score six-wicket IPL win over Indians
item.description: Travis Head reminds cricket fans of what he can produce with the bat, as he helps Sunrisers Hyderabad to a six-wicket win over Mumbai Indians.

item.title: Donald Trump's would-be assassin took hotel selfie while 'armed to the teeth', court told
item.description: Court documents reveal further details about the case against the alleged gunman at the White House correspondents' dinner in Washington.

item.title: Police say 'timeframe of survivability' ending for missing 5yo Sharon
item.description: After speaking to survival experts, NT police says the "timeframe of survivability" is coming to an end for a missing five-year-old girl in Alice Springs. The police commissioner also confirmed the search had been complicated by the fact the child is non-verbal.

item.title: Does the 'Jessica' tantrum trend really work?
item.description: Whether it's at home or in the middle of a shopping centre, tantrums can test the patience of any parent or caregiver.

item.title: Bodycam footage shows police officers subduing London attacker
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Melbourne teacher charged after camera found in primary school bathroom
item.description: The 33-year-old was arrested at the school in the city's south-east after the discovery of a camera in a bathroom.

item.title: Archibald Packing Room Prize 2026 won by Sean Layh for portrait of Jacob Collins
item.description: Sean Layh has won the $3,000 Packing Room Prize at this year's Archibald Prize for his portrait of Jacob Collins, titled The tragicall historie of Hamlet, Prince of Denmarke.

item.title: Miranda Priestly makes a splashy return in The Devil Wears Prada 2
item.description: The Devil Wears Prada 2 drops the beloved (or loathed) characters from 20 years ago into a very different media environment.

item.title: Ross Lyon clarifies Saints resignation offer over comment to First Nations players
item.description: St Kilda coach Ross Lyon is proud of the way the club reacted after he offended a group of the club's First Nations players.

item.title: What is inflation and should you worry about it?
item.description: The latest inflation data is making headlines. Find out what it means for you in three minutes.

item.title: Counterterrorism intelligence's share of funding in 'significant' decline since 2020, royal commission finds
item.description: Royal commissioner Virginia Bell says classified documents reveal a significant shift in intelligence spending away from counterterrorism since 2020, in the first findings of what failings led to last year's Bondi terror attack.

item.title: Tense exchanges in court as judge turns screws on ACCC's case against Woolworths
item.description: There have been tense exchanges in the Federal Court, with the judge overseeing the bombshell case between the ACCC and Woolworths questioning the consumer watchdog's case.

item.title: Crows' skipper Jordan Dawson ruled out for Showdown after attending brother's funeral
item.description: Adelaide Crow's skipper Jordan Dawson has been ruled out of Friday's Showdown following his brother's sudden death.

item.title: Western Queensland's Pilungah Reserve third to gain special status ending mineral prospecting permits
item.description: Pilungah Reserve, south-west of Mount Isa, has become Queensland's third special wildlife reserve, barring any future mining activity.

item.title: Parents of two children killed in Bowen Mountain house fire thank first responders and community
item.description: A statement shared by the parents of two children killed in a Bowen Mountain house fire say their "family's world has been forever changed".

item.title: Victorian parliamentary inquiry into summer fires hears emotional pleas from Harcourt residents
item.description: A parliamentary inquiry into the devastating bushfires across Victoria over the summer hears the emotional plea of those impacted.

item.title: Rebel Wilson says social media posts are true during defamation trial cross-examination
item.description: Rebel Wilson tells a court several social media posts targeting the lead actor on her film The Deb are all true, on her final day of cross-examination in a defamation trial.

item.title: Global Sumud Flotilla carrying Australians intercepted on its way to Gaza by IDF
item.description: The Global Sumud Flotilla has been intercepted by the IDF, an official tweet by the group says.

item.title: NSW Police under scrutiny over resources allocated to targeted Bondi Beach Hanukkah event
item.description: NSW Police officers assigned to patrol the Hanukkah celebration targeted in the Bondi Beach terrorist attack were told they did not need to "stay the entire duration" of the event, despite members of the Jewish community fearing it was at risk.

item.title: RACQ denies misleading customers with insurance notices amid ASIC Federal Court lawsuit
item.description: When the corporate regulator accused RACQ of misleading customers with insurance renewal notices, the insurer apologised. Now, it is defending the notices in court filings.

item.title: Chalmers does little to stop capital gains tax debate as budget looms
item.description: Seven years ago, proposed changes to capital gains tax and negative gearing were named as part of the reason Labor lost the unlosable election. Times have changed.

item.title: Savers Australia denies allegations of bed bugs at Brunswick thrift store
item.description: Thrift store chain Savers Australia has denied there is evidence of bed bugs at their popular Brunswick shop, as allegations of an outbreak continue to circulate online.

item.title: Man accused of attending Mount Gambier businesses while nude faces court
item.description: A Mount Gambier man is granted bail after he was allegedly nude while in businesses and on a busy main road three times in the past week.

item.title: Moore goals after siren as Hawks and Pies settle for thrilling draw
item.description: A goal after the siren from Dylan Moore allows Hawthorn to claim a draw against Collingwood at the MCG.

item.title: Thursday finance with Daniel Ziffer
item.description: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

item.title: WA Police seize guns from home with Nazi flags prominently displayed
item.description: Police raid a home as part of an operation to reassess licensed gun holders in the wake of the Bondi Beach shooting.

item.title: Community in mourning after 5yo girl's body found
item.description: Tributes are flowing in Alice Springs for a five-year-old Aboriginal girl whose body was found earlier today, as the community mourns her death.

item.title: Australia in talks over latest US proposal to open Strait of Hormuz, but PM says 'no determination' made
item.description: Australia has begun discussions with the United States about joining the latest US proposal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, as the Trump administration tries to build an international coalition to restore freedom of navigation in the critical waterway.

item.title: How two 'Origin plays' turned the tide for New South Wales against Queensland
item.description: With Queensland on the verge of another classic ambush, it took two "Origin plays" for New South Wales to turn the tide in the women's series opener in Newcastle.

item.title: Alice Springs erupts in violence after 5yo girl's alleged killer arrested
item.description: Wild scenes erupted on the streets of Alice Springs last night following the arrest of Jefferson Lewis over the alleged murder of five-year-old Kumanjayi Little Baby.

item.title: Seasonal produce for all your autumn soups, roasts and salads
item.description: From carrots to cauliflower, here is some of the best seasonal produce to enjoy in your autumn soups, roasts and salads.

item.title: Bond yields at crisis level as Australian government's 10-Year Treasury rate passes 5 per cent
item.description: The 10-Year Treasury bond, which the Commonwealth issues to raise money, pushes past 5 per cent, raising the cost of paying off debt and suggesting inflation is not under control.

item.title: The home battery rebate is changing. Is it still worth it and what's the right size?
item.description: In less than nine months, more than a quarter of a million batteries were installed in Australian households. Here's how the battery rebate is changing and why.

item.title: ACCC case against Woolworths winds up as spotlight remains on integrity of grocery pricing
item.description: As the Woolies federal court case wraps up and the Coles matter awaits judgment, here's what we've learned so far from the high-stakes cases.

item.title: In a normal political world, the Liberal Party would be expecting a Nepean by-election win
item.description: With no Labor candidate contesting this weekend's Nepean by-election, it should be a cakewalk for the Victorian Liberals. But we are no longer in a normal political world.

item.title: Cole Tomas Allen to remain in jail after alleged attempt to storm correspondents' dinner
item.description: Cole Tomas Allen, 31, appears briefly in court, days after he allegedly attempted to storm the White House Correspondents' Association dinner armed with guns and knives.

item.title: The Bondi royal commission interim report is careful but ultimately damning
item.description: The message beneath the restrained language of the report investigating the Bondi terror attack is that by Hanukkah 2025, Australia had grown dangerously complacent about terrorism – and about the safety of the Jewish community.

item.title: Commanders to brief Trump on fresh military options for Iran war — as it happened
item.description: Iran says it will respond with "long and painful strikes" on US positions if Washington renews attacks and reasserts its control over the Strait of Hormuz — as it happened.

item.title: New photo of Aung San Suu Kyi released as military says she is moved from prison to house arrest
item.description: Myanmar's detained former president Aung San Suu Kyi has been moved from prison to house arrest, according to state media.

item.title: National average house prices rose slightly in April, the slowest pace of growth since January 2025
item.description: Momentum in the housing market has slowed, with every capital city recording a slower pace of growth in April.

item.title: China using a double-insurance strategy to secure crude oil supplies amid Iran war
item.description: China relies heavily on Middle Eastern oil imports, but experts say a double-insurance strategy appears to have helped the country absorb at least the initial shock after disruption to the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Pacific nations aim to tackle regional unrest with expanded Pacific Response Group
item.description: The Pacific Response Group is beginning negotiations on a treaty that would allow it to respond more quickly to natural disasters and other crises across the region.

item.title: Once-safe Coalition seat Farrer now in sights of One Nation at by-election
item.description: A fascinating four-cornered contest is brewing as voters in the southern NSW seat of Farrer head to the ballot box for the second time in 12 months.

item.title: Mount Isa City Council moves to sell homes over unpaid rates in outback mining city
item.description: In Mount Isa, nearly 50 properties, some abandoned, have accrued years of unpaid rates and could be forced to sale.

item.title: ASX rebounds after seven losing sessions led by miners, ANZ shares slump despite profit jump — as it happened
item.description: The ASX rebounds after seven losing sessions but ANZ's profit announcement drags down the banks.

item.title: Victoria Police doubles recruit intake to 1,300 a year as it tries to fill hundreds of vacancies
item.description: The police force is trying to fill a shortfall in frontline officers with crime in Victoria at a 10-year high.

item.title: Josh Hazlewood smashed for 24 off one over as Shubman Gill fires in the IPL
item.description: Shubman Gill takes Australian quick Josh Hazlewood to task as Gujarat took down Bengaluru in the IPL.

item.title: Ashley Youth Detention Centre cameras offline during lockdown incident
item.description: Security cameras at Ashley Youth Detention Centre were offline when a detainee threatened a staff member with a knife during a cooking workshop, documents reveal.

item.title: Left in the red dirt: The forgotten trail of Australia's longest strike
item.description: It's been 80 years since Aboriginal workers in the Pilbara up-ended a slave-like labour system. The descendants of those involved fear its legacy is being forgotten.

item.title: Reza Adib, partner of former Queensland premier Annastacia Palaszczuk, charged with rape
item.description: Vahid Reza Adib, the partner of former Queensland premier Annastacia Palaszczuk, has been charged with three counts of rape relating to alleged offending against a woman in her 30s at Burleigh Heads on March 30.

item.title: US Congress approves bill to end record Homeland Security funding shutdown
item.description: "It is about damn time," said one Democrat as US politicians voted to end the funding freeze for most of the department's agencies after Mr Trump's deadly immigration operations in Minneapolis.

item.title: Britney Spears charged with driving under influence of alcohol and drugs
item.description: Britney Spears has been charged in California with driving under the influence of drugs and alcohol.

item.title: Construction ends on $915 million Parramatta Powerhouse museum set to open this year
item.description: Four years after work started, the highly anticipated Western Sydney museum is ready for the installation of five exhibitions, including a major show about space exploration.

item.title: Adelaide showed how good LIV Golf could be, but it was built on a flawed premise
item.description: While Adelaide showed just how good LIV Golf could be, the flawed premise of the breakaway tournament always spelled trouble.

item.title: Townsville man dies in fiery crash as road fatalities jump 24pc
item.description: Members of the public and emergency services assisted at the scene of a fatal crash in Townsville, one of several deadly crashes on Queensland roads over the past week.

item.title: From Foo Fighters to Jessie Ware and Nine Inch Noize — these are April's best albums
item.description: Come for Dave Grohl and co's latest. Stay for club-facing collabs, lush disco, homegrown releases and a pair of Québécois aliens making left-field instrumental jams.

item.title: Future of Northern Territory ambulance service in doubt as paramedics reach breaking point
item.description: A woman was left to suffer a seizure for two hours due to ambulance services being overwhelmed by call-outs earlier this month. The organisation's new chief executive has said unless more resources are funded to ease demand, the service could collapse.

item.title: A deadline for Trump to end the Iran war is looming. He may seek to get around it
item.description: A deadline for US President Donald Trump to end the Iran war is about to expire. Here is what to expect.

item.title: Putin condemns Ukrainian drone strikes against oil refineries deep inside Russian territory
item.description: Ukraine says its drones have struck an oil refinery deep inside Russia, marking the latest in a series of attacks on oil facilities behind the front lines as Kyiv seeks to curb Moscow’s energy revenues used to fund the war.

item.title: Guy Mont Spelling Bee intro 1
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Ricky Stuart says it is time Craig Bellamy put himself first after the NRL coach's shock diagnosis
item.description: Raiders coach Ricky Stuart says Craig Bellamy needs to start putting himself first after the Storm revealed the coaching legend had been diagnosed with a neurological condition.

item.title: More IP, more content, more jobs: What we learned at Screen Forever 40
item.description: Now in its 40th year, the annual Screen Forever conference attracts hundreds of industry leaders to look at the past, present and future of Australian screen content.

item.title: SA Police confirm remains found at Inman Valley belong to Jeff Mundy
item.description: SA Police say remains found in bushland south of Adelaide a week ago have been identified as belonging to Jeff Mundy, who was last seen by his family five years ago.

item.title: Trump not keen on wearing body armour as Secret Service says agent not shot by friendly fire
item.description: US President Donald Trump has declined to wear a bulletproof vest amid fresh concerns about his safety following a shooting at a gala dinner on Saturday.

item.title: Wollongong tradesman jailed for robbing paedophile in Lightning Ridge heist
item.description: A Wollongong man drove to Lightning Ridge with his police officer friend to rob an elderly convicted paedophile of $100,000.

item.title: Family of allegedly murdered 5yo calls for calm as police reveal vigilante attack left suspect unconscious
item.description: The family of Alice Springs five-year-old Kumanjayi Little Baby have called for calm following rioting in the outback town overnight, while police say her alleged murderer was beaten unconscious.

item.title: Suns coach Damien Hardwick fires back at Saints' Ross Lyon's AFL draft comment
item.description: Gold Coast coach Damien Hardwick provides a spirited response to St Kilda counterpart Ross Lyon's comment about the AFL national draft.

item.title: Port Pirie locals worried about jobs as Nyrstar smelter's future in doubt
item.description: Locals say the possible closure of the Port Pirie smelter and the loss of the 900 jobs associated with it would have ongoing effects on the city and the wider Spencer Gulf region.

item.title: Review finds fire ant eradication can succeed if flaws in 'mistrust and misinformation' are fixed
item.description: An independent review finds fire ant eradication is still achievable, but major issues, including poor communication, public mistrust and strategy confusion, must be fixed first.

item.title: Former ACT chief magistrate Lorraine Walker talks to Stateline
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Retiring ACT chief magistrate Lorraine Walker on mistakes, rehabilitation and compassion
item.description: Heart is not something anyone expects from the court system, but it was something outgoing ACT chief magistrate Lorraine Walker, who retired in March after 15 years in the job, aimed to portray.

item.title: Rebel Wilson defamation trial told Charlotte MacInnes was 'fudging' her version of producer incident
item.description: Rebel Wilson's defamation trial hears from another producer on her directorial debut, who claims The Deb's lead actor was "fudging" her version of events about an alleged incident during production.

item.title: NT government criticised for snubbing youth justice and incarceration Senate inquiry
item.description: The inquiry heard calls for more scrutiny over how Commonwealth money is spent in the NT, which is trending backwards on seven Closing the Gap targets.

item.title: May temperature records fall across Victoria as unseasonably warm weather continues
item.description: Weather records fall across the state, as a spell of summer-like conditions tips into the first day of May.

item.title: Crows claim epic after two goals inside final minute as Freo downs Dogs
item.description: Adelaide claims a remarkable one-point victory over Port Adelaide after Hayden Young fires the Dockers to victory over the Bulldogs.

item.title: Friday Finance with David Chau
item.description: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

item.title: Chinese-owned Landbridge launches international legal action over forced sale of Port of Darwin
item.description: Anthony Albanese pledged at the federal election that Labor would return the port to Australian hands, saying it necessary for national security.

item.title: UK police charge man with stabbing attack on two Jewish Londoners
item.description: UK police charge a 45-year-old man over the stabbing of two Jewish men in north London, the latest in a string of attacks targeting Britain's Jewish community.

item.title: Six Australians among Gaza aid flotilla activists taken to Crete after Israeli interception
item.description: More than 100 activists, including six Australians, on ships bound for Gaza were intercepted by Israeli forces earlier this week.

item.title: Tie property tax changes to income tax cuts, productivity chief says
item.description: Productivity Commission chair Danielle Wood says there is a "case" for property tax changes, but for it to be serious reform, the economic benefit must flow to workers.

item.title: Trump rejects Iran's new proposal to end the war, threatening to 'blast them to hell' if deal not reached
item.description: Peace talks between the United States and Iran have stalled, with US President Donald Trump threatening to "blast them to hell" if they don't reach a deal.

item.title: A guide to the 2026 Stella Prize shortlist, featuring Geraldine Brooks, Tasma Walton and more
item.description: A memoir by Geraldine Brooks and a historical novel by actor-turned-author Tasma Walton are among the eclectic works up for one of Australia's most prestigious literary awards.

item.title: Four for the weekend: Apex, Margo's Got Money Troubles and a massaman curry
item.description: What to watch, read, listen to and eat this weekend, according to our culture and food teams.

item.title: Deep fractures in the Gulf over Iran have implications for everyone, including Australia
item.description: Donald Trump's earlier signalling that he will neither walk away from the war declaring a victory or escalate it, but will try to starve the Iranians out, suggests there is no clear sign of how this war could end.

item.title: Iran and US play 'game of chicken' as regime's oil stockpiled inside blockade
item.description: Analysis of how much oil Iran is producing, shipping and having to store shows pressure is mounting on Tehran, but while experts agree the US blockade is effective, there are questions about whether it can really force Iran back to the negotiating table.

item.title: Gas vs beer: How David Pocock's 57-second video caused the government a weeks-long tax headache
item.description: David Pocock was struggling to find cut-through on the issue of gas taxes, but then a question about the beer excise sparked a social media war of words.

item.title: What is a town camp? The site of Kumanjayi Little Baby's tragic alleged abduction
item.description: Kumanjayi Little Baby is believed to have been taken from her bed at night while visiting a town camp near Alice Springs with her mother. This is how town camps came to be.

item.title: Secrecy, 'manipulation' and a long interest in guns: Sajid Akram's road to Bondi
item.description: Sajid Akram was a man of unusual secrecy — even to his own family. New details uncovered by Background Briefing shed light on his downward spiral into violence.

item.title: What we know about the ISIS-linked Australian families in Syria
item.description: Four Australian women, their children and grandchildren are stuck in limbo in Syria after leaving a refugee camp for family members of suspected Islamic State fighters. This is what we know about them.

item.title: Albanese says US under Trump 'playing a different role' in world
item.description: Anthony Albanese says the United States is "playing a different role" in the world under President Donald Trump, forcing Australia and allies to adjust how they respond to unexpected conflicts.

item.title: Police criticised for using tasers on people in inappropriate and unnecessary situations
item.description: Disturbing cases of people wrongly tasered by police on their farm and at school has raised questions over whether officers are too reliant on the devices.

item.title: Road safety advocates want better import standards for large utes
item.description: Sales of large utes have increased by 270 per cent since 2019, data from a national transport safety body.

item.title: Alternative Suspension breaks cycle for repeatedly suspended students
item.description: Of the students who have attended the Alternative Suspension program, 67 per cent have received no further suspensions.

item.title: Fiji considers state of emergency over drugs and gangs as police and military crackdown ramps up
item.description: The suspected murder of a Fijian underworld figure held in military custody has sparked a new chapter in the island nation's wrestle with drug-related crimes, with the police and military combining forces for a highly visible "crackdown".

item.title: Why Taiwan is becoming a hotspot for Australian travellers
item.description: Lots of Australians travel to Taiwan chasing flavour and wanting to try the best beef noodle soup — but they leave wanting to explore more of its history and nature.

item.title: Starc takes 3 wickets on IPL return to halt Delhi Capitals' losing run, Labuschagne leads Hyderabad to PSL final
item.description: Mitchell Starc picked up three wickets on his first bowl since January's BBL final, reviving Delhi Capitals, while Marnus Labuschagne led Hyderabad United to the PSL final.

item.title: SA Liberals poised to hold firm in upper house as Dennis Hood rejects presidency role
item.description: Labor's path to getting legislation through the upper house may get slightly more difficult after Liberal MLC Dennis Hood — who was strongly linked to the upper house presidency role — said he would remain on the floor of parliament.

item.title: Mornington Peninsula voters head to polls for Nepean by-election
item.description: The by-election has already had a high turnout of early voters, with the Victorian Electoral Commission saying about half the electorate had already voted by Friday night.

item.title: Iranian and FIFA football officials to meet ahead of World Cup games in USA
item.description: Iranian soccer officials are set to meet with FIFA in Zurich about their FIFA World Cup games in the United States in June, with Iran's football boss saying there are "many issues to discuss".

item.title: Australian cows help rebuild Indonesian dairy herds after foot-and-mouth outbreak
item.description: An Australian dairy program in Indonesia is helping farmers in West Java boost production after a devastating foot-and-mouth disease outbreak.

item.title: Australia's south-east basks in record May temperatures, but polar weather blast days away
item.description: Just weeks from winter, and south-east Australia is still enjoying summertime warmth.

item.title: 'Girl power movie', Bondi bathtub and Russian oligarch: Inside Rebel Wilson's defamation trial
item.description: Charlotte MacInnes and Rebel Wilson are locked in a messy defamation battle involving disparaging social media posts, a "Russian oligarch" and sexual harassment accusations.

item.title: Inquiry into Queensland CFMEU branch hears of union's impact on construction of Brisbane's Cross River Rail
item.description: Queensland's inquiry into the CFMEU has revealed Cross River Rail was plagued by years of interference. Here are the key takeaways from this week's hearings.

item.title: Fifty years ago, music was on the brink of major change with seismic arrival of punk rock to centre stage
item.description: The music industry is always in a state of flux, but what was happening in 1976 was akin to the dinosaurs looking up and seeing a meteor on the way.

item.title: Why portrait painting still matters in the age of the selfie
item.description: What began as an awkward pose for the Archibald Prize became a meditation on why portrait painting still matters in the age of the iPhone.

item.title: Oscar Piastri third for Miami Sprint Race and Lando Norris on pole
item.description: Lando Norris starts the Miami Grand Prix sprint race on pole position, with world championship leader Kimi Antonelli denying Oscar Piastri a spot on the front row of the grid.

item.title: Number of Tasmania Police officers off duty due to mental health doubles in five years
item.description: More than 100 Tasmanian police officers are currently off duty due to mental health compensation claims, a number that has doubled in five years.

item.title: Removing the bund at Cape Palmerston National Park
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: How salt water is restoring life to parts of the Queensland coast
item.description: More than half a century ago, lush wetlands along the Queensland coastline were cut off from the ocean as farmers built physical barriers to stop salt water flowing into their land. Now, conservation groups and traditional owners are restoring tidal flows and bringing life back to the land.

item.title: Pub and restaurant latest targets in series of Melbourne arson attacks
item.description: A man has been shot at during an aggravated home invasion, and a pub and restaurant were set on fire overnight in Melbourne.

item.title: World Athletics president Sebastian Coe says super shoe technology should be enabled as records tumble
item.description: Lord Sebastian Coe says World Athletics has a duty to "enable" super shoe development despite concerns.

item.title: WA government 'confident' state has enough fuel for May as Middle East conflict drags on
item.description: WA's energy minister says the state will have the fuel it needs for this month and has "growing confidence" for June, as further details are released about 10 renewable energy projects that are set to be built across regional WA.

item.title: US to withdraw 5,000 troops from Germany amid Trump spat with Merz
item.description: The United States is withdrawing 5,000 troops from NATO ally Germany amid a growing a rift between the nations' leaders over the Iran war.

item.title: Ratko Mladić's lawyers say 'Butcher of Bosnia' near death and should be released
item.description: In a submission to a UN court, Ratko Mladić's lawyers say he has suffered a serious medical incident and should be released from The Hague on humanitarian grounds.

item.title: Meekatharra bike donation and workshop gets kids cycling to school
item.description: The Meekatharra community comes together to restore donated bikes, making a visible difference at the local school.

item.title: This pumpkin soup is comfort food with a twist that takes it to the next level - ABC News
item.description: Pumpkin soup is one of a few meals with just one main ingredient. This delicious version spices things up with coconut cream and an easy curry-leaf oil.

item.title: Why Dee Salmin would rather be a 'feminist witch' than a 'cool girl'
item.description: Podcaster Dee Salmin says people should steer clear of traditional dating advice and focus more on themselves.

item.title: Conservation work helps endangered flora recover in Stirling Range
item.description: Seed collections taken before major bushfires in WA's Stirling Range National Park give hope for the continued survival of flora species found nowhere else in the world.

item.title: Australians likely to pay more for cosmetic items even if Strait of Hormuz reopens soon. Here's why
item.description: Australians spend about $450 on average on beauty products per year. That could soon rise as petroleum prices increase.

item.title: Carlton blows another half-time lead as Voss pins blame on defensive effort
item.description: Carlton coach Michael Voss has said his side's defence is not up to standard after the Blues suffered another second-half collapse on Saturday.

item.title: Storms, rare fogbows and spectacular sunsets — see WA's best weather photos for April
item.description: In classic shoulder season conditions, April delivered a transitional mix of warm beach weather and cool, wet days with rainfall in the double-digits as we inch closer to winter.

item.title: Student rocket team successfully launches Galah rocket 10,000 feet in NSW outback
item.description: Students with space industry aspirations successfully launch a rocket 10,000 feet in the air in regional Australia.

item.title: CCTV footage captures man attacking nun in Jerusalem
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Israeli police arrest man suspected of attacking nun near Jerusalem's Old City
item.description: Israeli police release a video showing what eyewitnesses say was an attack by a Jewish man on a nun near Jerusalem's Old City. Police say the suspect, a 36-year-old man, was later arrested.

item.title: Man airlifted with life-threatening injuries after reports of caravan park brawl involving knives
item.description: A man has been airlifted to hospital with life-threatening injuries after being stabbed during a wild brawl at a caravan park in a small Victorian town.

item.title: Who's really in charge in Iran?
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Emotional scenes as Rooster returns after 1,337 days out with illness
item.description: The Roosters hang on for a 38-24 win over the Broncos, as Cody Ramsey plays his first NRL game in almost four years.

item.title: Volunteers say new Crime Stoppers Qld model to strengthen statewide reach and impact will not work
item.description: Public safety charity Crime Stoppers Queensland will undergo significant changes to its community outreach model, which volunteers describe as "a slap in the face".

item.title: WA's energy minister sits down for a Stateline interview to discuss the state's fuel supply.
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Spirit of Tasmania V arrives in Geelong as preparation for Bass Strait voyages continues
item.description: Following a seven-week journey from Scotland, the Spirit of Tasmania V has arrived in Geelong. It will undergo gantry testing tomorrow before being temporarily relocated to Station Pier in Melbourne.

item.title: Charges yet to be laid as Kumanjayi Little Baby's family thanks community for support
item.description: The family of five-year-old Kumanjayi Little Baby, whose body was found on the outskirts of Alice Springs on Thursday, has thanked the community for their support as tributes flow in from across the country.

item.title: Counting underway in Nepean by-election, with Liberal Party looking to retain Victorian state seat
item.description: The Liberal Party seeks to retain the seat vacated by former Victorian deputy Liberal leader Sam Groth.

item.title: Brisbane Lions coach Chris Fagan slams 'hero' parent after Essendon whiteboard leak
item.description: Chris Fagan has expressed his disappointment after the leaking of confidential Brisbane Lions notes on Essendon players.

item.title: Anthony Marsh wins Nepean by-election, retaining seat for Liberal Party
item.description: Anthony Marsh wins the Nepean by-election, retaining the seat for the Liberal Party.

item.title: Quiz: Welcome to your Sunday serve of Guy Montgomery's spelling stumpers
item.description: You lot take your spelling seriously. Repeat after me: It's just an internet quiz. It's just an internet quiz.

item.title: ODYs_SteeringWheelEve_2904
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: It costs $2,000 for Sean to get to Sydney and back.
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: ODYs_HumeHighway_2904
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: It will take about nine hours for Sean to get to Sydney.
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Trucking up the Hume as diesel prices boom
item.description: On Australia's busiest freight route, it takes four shots of coffee, $2,553 of fuel and a little hair metal to keep the wheels of the economy turning.

item.title: One of James Valentine's craziest ideas was one where he said nothing at all
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Radio star James Valentine shares what he learnt in his 'year of living gratefully'
item.description: Broadcaster James Valentine was offered two different treatments for his cancer, so he chose the one that let him keep doing what he loved. Australian Story followed Valentine over his final 12 months as he faced the end with courage, purpose and humour.

item.title: Scam centre survivors criticise Steam video games that closely depict real-life compounds
item.description: Scam centre survivors say they are hurt by games that serialise scam compound experiences online, but one creator says he hopes people find the game "fun".

item.title: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: King Charles won over Donald Trump with his US visit but not progressive mayor Zohran Mamdani
item.description: King Charles's state visit to the United States hit a bump in New York when Mayor Zohran Mamdani said if the pair were to speak, he would "probably encourage him to return the Koh-i-Noor diamond".

item.title: Australian taxpayers also take on risk for major oil and gas projects. Why is that ignored?
item.description: Australian governments take on risk to get major resource projects up and running, so why don't they take an equity stake?

item.title: The year that changed the shape of Albanese's agenda
item.description: The prime minister's second term is now defined less by what he wants to do than by what unforeseen events will allow.

item.title: What are we losing when big business buys up our favourite little pubs?
item.description: From a tiny Riverina pub to a polished inner-city institution, Australia's local pubs are changing hands at record pace. As big money pours in, what happens to the communities that built them?

item.title: How the kindness of strangers helped this Ukrainian family after fleeing war
item.description: Wildlife veterinarian Maria Mykytiuk led an idyllic life in Bucha, Ukraine, before Russian forces invaded the city in 2022.

item.title: Robert Forster from The Go-Betweens on his debut novel, Songwriters on the Run
item.description: The Go-Betweens frontman spent years developing the story, which follows a pair of musicians on the run from the law as they try to clear their names.

item.title: Physical and mental wellbeing program seeks to help veterans adjust to life after military service
item.description: When training and physical fitness are a part of the job, sometimes veterans can drop off when they discharge, to the detriment of their mental and physical wellbeing.
